summary: Wired the previously process-only draft content gate into the runtime UI and strengthened the not-medical-advice framing. REASSURE_CONTENT.status in content/kb.ts was already the clinician sign-off flag but gated nothing at runtime; a new pure helper (src/features/reassure/domain/contentGate.ts, clinicalContentVisible(isDevBuild)) now hides the placeholder clinical KB blocks from public (non-__DEV__) builds until status flips to 'approved'. In AnswerCard, gated topic answers keep the calm bounded card shape but replace the clinical line/normal/helps/call blocks with non-clinical review-pending copy plus a pediatrician pointer; the 'Common tonight' TopicAccordion (which renders every KB topic) is hidden on the screen. Triage escalation ('call your pediatrician'), the non-medical guides, and the code-computed recap are deliberately NOT gated — pointing at a real professional is always the safe direction. The screen subtitle now reads 'General supportive information for tonight — not medical advice, never a diagnosis' (the existing bounded-promise card and quiet footer disclaimer were already in place and are untouched). Audited marketing surfaces (paywall, upgrade card, onboarding, hero, AI consent card): no AI-doctor/pediatrician claims exist; the AI consent card already discloses 'general information, not medical advice'. Three new smoke checks (RG1-RG3) pin the gate logic, both render sites, triage staying ungated, and the disclaimer copy. No KB/red-flag strings were changed (edge-function mirror untouched, X17 still green); no new AI system, dependencies, or architecture.
validation_notes: npx tsc --noEmit: clean (exit 0). npm run check:local-interactions: all 379 checks passed (376 pre-existing + 3 new RG checks; X13/X14/X15/X17/X17b-e, PC and X24 Reassure guards all still green). npm run lint (expo lint): exit 0, no warnings.
manual_followups: Clinician review remains the real launch gate: work docs/reassure-content-review.md items 1-15, then flip REASSURE_CONTENT.status to 'approved' with reviewedBy/reviewedAt (smoke X15 enforces the shape) — the gated UI then shows the KB content automatically in public builds.

QA a release-mode build (not Expo dev) to see the gated surface exactly as an Apple reviewer would: topic asks should show the review-pending card, the Common-tonight accordion should be absent, and triage should still offer the pediatrician call.
REVIEW_PENDING_LINE / REVIEW_PENDING_BODY in AnswerCard.tsx are new (non-clinical) UX copy — added as item 15 in the review manifest; have the copy owner glance at the wording.
apple_review_risk_remaining: Reviewer-facing App Store metadata (app description, screenshots, review notes) is outside the repo — ensure it does not describe Reassure as medical guidance or an AI doctor and mentions the general-information disclaimer.

In a public build with draft content, the example chips still include topic asks (e.g. 'Hiccups after feeds') that now resolve to the review-pending card — safe and honest, but a reviewer may note the feature answers few questions; acceptable for the local-only v1 posture.

The AI night-read (Pro + consent + kill-switch gated, off by default in beta env posture) still exists in code; if a reviewer build ever enables it, its copy is disclaimed but the feature would draw Guideline 5.1/2.1 attention — keep it dark for v1 submission.
